taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Data Engineering, Data Engineer
core_skills: Data Engineering, Backend Engineering, Data Pipelines, Pipeline Orchestration, Apache Airflow
skill_match_scores: Data Engineering:1.0, Backend Engineering:1.0, Data Pipelines:0.5, Pipeline Orchestration:0.0, Apache Airflow:0.0
must_have_quals: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Math, or a related field (or equivalent experience), 4+ years of data engineering or backend engineering experience with a focus on pipelines, orchestration, or platform, Hands-on experience building and maintaining production data pipelines (e.g. Airflow, Prefect, Luigi, or similar). We use Python for our pipeline environment, machine learning, and developer tooling; we don't require Python expertise and are happy for you to learn on the job, Experience with cloud object storage and data-at-scale (we use AWS and S3; cloud experience is required, but prior AWS experience is not), Comfort with Kubernetes and container-based deployments in practice: running workloads on K8s, resource and volume configuration, and debugging pod/worker issues, Ability to own work end-to-end: design, implement, test, and operate pipelines and related tooling. You are comfortable picking up new parts of the stack when needed, Strong collaboration and communication; you work well with ML, hardware, and product stakeholders and can explain tradeoffs clearly
preferred_quals: Experience in early-stage or fast-moving environments where scope and ownership evolve, Experience with Apache Airflow (especially 3.x) and/or Astronomer, Experience with geospatial data, imagery, lidar, or point clouds, Interest in utilities, forestry, or field operations and how data pipelines support those domains
core_responsibilities: Design, build, and maintain data pipelines at scale, Develop and evolve DAGs that orchestrate complex, multi-step workflows, Work closely with our in-house ML team and coordinate with our hardware team on ingestion and formats, Improve DAG design and execution, resource and cost tuning, reliability and observability, Contribute to how we run Airflow and Kubernetes in the cloud, Work alongside ML engineers, hardware engineers, and software engineers, Turn a technically complex set of requirements into a rich data set, Partner closely with some of the largest utilities in the country and contribute to efforts to develop new workflows
job_summary: Treeswift is a company that empowers energy companies to modernize their field work through advanced data solutions. The Data Platform Engineer will design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines, collaborating with various teams to enhance the data platform and its capabilities.
